sources_scanned.name: arXiv:2507.21046 — Self-Evolving Agents Survey
sources_scanned.finding: Formal taxonomy: What/When/How/Where. Swarm maps to all 4 axes. Identifies continual learning but not continual forgetting.

sources_scanned.name: EvoAgentX/Awesome-Self-Evolving-Agents
sources_scanned.finding: 300+ papers cataloged. Memory optimization listed but only accumulation direction (A-MEM, Memento, Memory-R1). No compaction.

sources_scanned.name: dsifry/metaswarm
sources_scanned.finding: Closest structural analog: Claude Code multi-agent, 18 agents, recursive orchestration, JSONL knowledge. Key differences: hierarchical vs stigmergic, designed vs operated.

sources_scanned.name: kyegomez/swarms
sources_scanned.finding: Enterprise orchestration framework. Production-focused, not self-evolving. No knowledge persistence mechanism.

sources_scanned.name: HKUDS/ClawTeam
sources_scanned.finding: Finance-focused agent swarm with portfolio manager spawning analysts. Domain-specific, not general self-evolution.

sources_scanned.name: numberanalytics.com — Stigmergy: The Future of Decentralized AI
sources_scanned.finding: Stigmergy recognized as coordination mechanism for decentralized AI. No implementations cited beyond robotics.

novel_swarm_contributions: Knowledge compaction/decay (compact.py, proxy-K) — no external analog found
Git-native substrate for concurrent coordination (merge conflicts = collision detection)
532-session operational history — most external systems are designed but not operated
Expect-act-diff as primary learning loop — distinct from plan-driven or RL approaches
Human-as-participant vs human-in-the-loop — unique authority model
swarm_gaps_vs_external: No model-level self-improvement (swarm sits above LLM, cannot modify substrate)
No automated multi-agent construction (agents are session-instances, not specialized)
No formal workflow DAG — stigmergic coordination is powerful but harder to debug
outcome: POSITIVE — external landscape validates swarm approach, compaction gap is novel
